headlines.title: Senior official ousted over Mandelson security row to face MPs
headlines.description: Former Former Office chief Sir Olly Robbins is expected to be grilled by the Foreign Affairs Committee on Tuesday.

headlines.title: Chris Mason: Mandelson saga is a messy palaver - and the questions continue to swirl
headlines.description: Sir Keir and the top official at the Foreign Office at the time are expected to face questions from MPs next week.

headlines.title: Mortgage rates show signs of falling after Iran war peak
headlines.description: Major lenders make rate reductions as markets take some heart from a possible truce in the Iran war.

headlines.title: 'I'm the lucky one' - more than one in three young men now live with their parents
headlines.description: Last year, the highest proportion of men aged 20-34 were still living at home since at least 2007 as the rising cost of living takes hold.

headlines.title: Pregnancy vaccine reduces baby hospital admissions for RSV by 80%
headlines.description: A study confirms the vaccine gives excellent protection for babies against life-threatening chest infections.

headlines.title: The home cooks reviving forgotten British dishes you've probably never heard of
headlines.description: Carrageen pudding and Bedfordshire clangers might no longer be household staples, but some home chefs are bringing them back.

headlines.title: Coachella campers are turning tents fancy and not all Brits are happy
headlines.description: Campers are being judged for over-the-top tent set ups at this year's festival in California.

headlines.title: My weekly juggling act - being a teacher to other children and a mum to my own
headlines.description: Many teachers say they struggle to balance the pressures of their job with the demands of being a parent.

headlines.title: Orbán's era was over in a flash and Hungary's next PM is a man in a hurry
headlines.description: Péter Magyar and his Tisza party are wasting no time preparing for the transfer of power after their dramatic landslide victory.

headlines.title: A man wrongfully served 17 years for rape. Now another man has been convicted
headlines.description: Malkinson had his rape conviction quashed in 2023 after fresh DNA analysis linked a new suspect to the offence.

headlines.title: What people in power think the impact of the Iran war will be
headlines.description: I have been chatting with some of the world's most powerful people about the conflict and the economy. Here's what they said.

headlines.title: A moment of risk and opportunity: How two ceasefires could boost US-Iran talks
headlines.description: Some kind of diplomatic progress is now under way, but is it enough to prevent a return to war?

headlines.title: UK and France to lead defensive mission in Strait of Hormuz
headlines.description: Sir Keir Starmer has said further details of the plan will come after a meeting in London next week.

headlines.title: Oil prices plunge as Iran says Strait of Hormuz 'open' during ceasefire
headlines.description: Brent crude sinks by a tenth after Iran says the key waterway is open for commercial ships for the rest of the ceasefire.

headlines.title: Swinney predicts SNP majority at Holyrood election
headlines.description: The first minister has told BBC News that his party will return at least 65 seats at next month's vote.

headlines.title: Starmer faces 'judgement day' and 'Breakthrough in the Strait'
headlines.description: Pressure is building on Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer as Lord Mandelson's vetting dominates Saturday's papers.

headlines.title: Irish fugitive and alleged crime boss Daniel Kinahan arrested in Dubai
headlines.description: Kinahan, in his 40s, was arrested in Dubai on foot of an arrest warrant issued by the Irish courts.

headlines.title: Japan comes up with new word for days hotter than 40C after blistering summer
headlines.description: The term - kokushobi - translates to "cruelly hot", "brutally hot" or "severely hot", and comes after Japan's hottest summer on record.

headlines.title: Fears over rogue parking by sunrise-chasers at national park after overnight ban
headlines.description: Authorities in Eryri want to crackdown on anti-social behaviour, but there are concerns people would park on local roads.

headlines.title: How Coventry earned Premier League return after 25 years
headlines.description: After a quarter of a century out of the Premier League, groundshares and administration, Frank Lampard has led Coventry City back to the top flight.

headlines.title: British man pleads guilty to conspiring to steal $8m in virtual currency
headlines.description: The Scottish 24-year-old admits defrauding at least a dozen companies and their employees across the US.

headlines.title: Sinatra fan Jimmy does it his way to keep promise to late wife
headlines.description: Jimmy Moir went to L.A. to see where his idol made his music - and ended up with a few surprises.

headlines.title: Boy donates lockdown locks to children's charity
headlines.description: Noah braves the scissors and gets a short do after growing out his hair for six years.

headlines.title: People assume I can't speak Welsh because I'm not white
headlines.description: Seren Jones says some Welsh speakers have switched to English when talking to her.

headlines.title: 'My sister loved to party and turned her life around' - the nun on the way to sainthood
headlines.description: Thousands attend a retreat in Derry named after Sister Clare, 10 years after her death in an earthquake in Ecuador.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Have the Democrats found a new strategy to scare Trump?
headlines.description: Why an oyster fisherman with a controversial past is splitting the Democrats

headlines.title: Mandelson Failed Security Vetting...The Prime Minister Speaks Out
headlines.description: Keir Starmer says it's 'staggering' he wasn’t told Mandelson failed security vetting.

headlines.title: Puss in Boots, voiced by Antonio Banderas, is on an exciting quest
headlines.description: Puss teams up with a fellow thieving feline and Humpty Dumpty to go on an exciting quest.

headlines.title: How Coventry earned Premier League return after 25 years
headlines.description: After a quarter of a century out of the Premier League, groundshares and administration, Frank Lampard has led Coventry City back to the top flight.

headlines.title: Fitzpatrick's wild birdie and superb 63 puts him in Heritage lead
headlines.description: Former US Open champion Matt Fitzpatrick has a one-shot lead over Viktor Hovland at the RBC Heritage in South Carolina.

headlines.title: Bournemouth close to appointing Rose
headlines.description: Bournemouth are close to appointing Marco Rose as Andoni Iraola's replacement as head coach.

headlines.title: From FA ban to double Euros winners -  Lionesses reach 500 games
headlines.description: BBC Sport takes a look at 11 defining moments in the history of England women before their milestone 500th official fixture on Saturday.

headlines.title: O'Sullivan chasing eighth Crucible title aged 50
headlines.description: In 2001, Ronnie O'Sullivan won his first World Snooker Championship. Now, a quarter of a century later, the Rocket is aiming for a record-breaking eighth title.

headlines.title: 'Mums thank me for changing the narrative': How motherhood in rugby has evolved
headlines.description: Lark Atkin-Davies and Abbie Ward - two of four pregnant England Women's Rugby World Cup winners - discuss how motherhood in rugby is changing.

headlines.title: Russell 'would understand' if Verstappen leaves F1
headlines.description: George Russell says Formula 1 "would not want to lose" Max Verstappen - but would "understand" if the four-time world champion decided to leave the sport.
